---
title: "Tiger Beetle Maps"
author: "EGP"
date: "2025-06-18"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
rm(list=ls())
graphics.off()
```
# Read-In Data
```{r}
full_data<-read.csv("spec_expanded.csv")
countries<-read.csv("countries.csv")
```
# Packages
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(broom)
library(viridis)
library(cowplot)
library(car)
library(moments)
library(gridExtra)
library(patchwork)
```
# Data Summaries
```{r}
full_data$spec_area_mm2 <- full_data$spec_area_mm2 %>%
  na_if("NONE") %>%
  na_if("MISSING") %>%
  na_if("") %>%
  as.numeric()

full_data$len1_mm <- full_data$len1_mm %>%
  na_if("NONE") %>%
  na_if("MISSING") %>%
  na_if("") %>%
  as.numeric()

full_data$len2_mm <- full_data$len2_mm %>%
  na_if("NONE") %>%
  na_if("MISSING") %>%
  na_if("") %>%
  as.numeric()

# Size distribution
summary(full_data$spec_area_mm2)

# Latitude distribution
summary(full_data$latitude)

```
## Summary Plots
### Body Size
#### Histogram
```{r}

ggplot(full_data, aes(x = spec_area_mm2)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 50, fill = "black", alpha = 0.7) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Body Size", 
       x = "Body Size (mm²)", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()

# Data probably need to be log-transformed due to HUGE Manticora

ggplot(full_data, aes(x = spec_area_mm2)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 50, fill = "red", alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Body Size (Log Scale)", 
       x = "Body Size (mm²) - Log Scale", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()

full_data$log10_size <- log10(full_data$spec_area_mm2)
```
#### QQplot
```{r}
qq_size<-ggplot(full_data, aes(sample = spec_area_mm2)) +
  stat_qq(alpha = 0.6) + stat_qq_line(color = "red") +
  labs(title = "D. Q-Q Plot: Original", 
       x = "Theoretical", y = "Sample") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 12))

qq_log10size<-ggplot(full_data, aes(sample = log10_size)) +
  stat_qq(alpha = 0.6) + stat_qq_line(color = "red") +
  labs(title = "E. Q-Q Plot: Log₁₀", 
       x = "Theoretical", y = "Sample") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 12))

grid.arrange(qq_size,qq_log10size, ncol = 2)

# I know Manticora will be massive outliers but I don't want to exclude them from the dataset as they represent true and interesting biological variation; genus will simply need to be included in the model
```

###Latitudes
```{r}
# our collection is quite northern hemisphere biased
hemisphere_summary <- full_data %>%
  mutate(hemisphere = ifelse(latitude >= 0, "Northern", "Southern")) %>%
  count(hemisphere) %>%
  mutate(percent = round(n / sum(n) * 100, 1))

print(hemisphere_summary)
```

####Latitude bands
```{r}
# temperate bias (over 30%), with decent sampling around the tropics N and S

full_data$lat_band <- cut(full_data$latitude, 
                              breaks = seq(-60, 60, by = 10),
                              labels = paste(seq(-60, 50, by = 10), "to", seq(-50, 60, by = 10)),
                              include.lowest = TRUE)

lat_band_summary <- full_data %>%
  count(lat_band) %>%
  mutate(percent = round(n / sum(n) * 100, 1)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(lat_band))

print(lat_band_summary)
```
####Histograms
```{r}
# raw latitude - again, showing temperate and N hem. bias
ggplot(full_data, aes(x = latitude)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.7, color = "white") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", linewidth = 1) +
  labs(
    title = "A. Raw Latitude Distribution",
    x = "Latitude (°)",
    y = "Number of Specimens",
    caption = "Red line = Equator"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 12))

```
###Genus
```{r}
# counts, mean/median, sd, variation, min/max, range, and mean/sd for log10 size

genus_summary <- full_data %>%
  group_by(genus) %>%
  summarise(
    n_specimens = n(),
    mean_size = mean(spec_area_mm2),
    median_size = median(spec_area_mm2),
    sd_size = sd(spec_area_mm2),
    cv_size = sd_size / mean_size,
    min_size = min(spec_area_mm2),
    max_size = max(spec_area_mm2),
    size_range = max_size - min_size,
    mean_log_size = mean(log10_size),
    sd_log_size = sd(log10_size),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(n_specimens))
print(genus_summary)
```

```{r}
# only use genera with at least 20 specimens

genera_20plus <- genus_summary %>%
  filter(n_specimens >= 20)

print(genera_20plus)

```
####Size ranges
```{r}
genera_sizes <- full_data %>%
  filter(genus %in% genera_20plus$genus) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(genus, spec_area_mm2, median), y = spec_area_mm2)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Size Distribution by Genus",
    x = "Genus",
    y = "Body Size (mm²) - Log Scale",
    caption = "Ordered by median size"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic", size = 10),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 12)
  )

plot(genera_sizes)
```
# Map
```{r}
# make bins for specimen counts by country (estimated from representative)
countrylist<-as.data.frame(table(countries$country_all))
countrylist<-rename(countrylist, admin=Var1)
countrylist<-rename(countrylist, freq=Freq)
bin<-as.numeric(cut(countrylist$freq,breaks = c(0,5,10,30,100,500,9000)))
countrylist<-cbind(countrylist,bin)
world <- ne_countries(scale = "medium", returnclass = "sf")
world<-left_join(world,countrylist,by='admin')
world<-world[-c(240),]

# points for known coordinates
sites <- countries %>%
  select(latitude, longitude)
sites<-distinct(sites)

```
## Plot Map
```{r}
map <- ggplot(data = world) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = bin)) + 
  scale_fill_viridis_c(
    option = "viridis",
    name = "count",
    breaks = c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6),
    labels = c("0", "5", "10", "30", "100", "500+")
  ) +
  geom_point(data = sites, aes(y = latitude, x = longitude), 
             size = 2, col = "black", alpha = .6, stroke = 0.1, 
             shape = 21, fill = "red") +
  theme_void() +
  theme(
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.text = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks = element_blank()
  )

plot(map)

```
